name: Leonard Feather
abstract: Leonard Geoffrey Feather (13 September 1914 – 22 September 1994) was a British-born jazz pianist, composer, and producer, who was best known for his music journalism and other writing.

concerts.label: Woody Herman and His Orchestra and The Nat King Cole Trio
Jazz for Moderns: Dave Brubeck Quartet, Four Freshman, Sonny Rollins Trio, and Maynard Ferguson and His Orchestra
Midnight Performance: Jazz for Moderns: Dave Brubeck Quartet, Four Freshman, Sonny Rollins Trio, and Maynard Ferguson and His Orchestra
Concert In Jazz: A Program of Modern American Music
Woody Herman and His Thundering Herd: 40th Anniversary Concert

about_quotes.speaker: Patricia Willard
about_quotes.evidence: [INTERVIEWER] According to Leonard Feather and John Chilton's Johnny Shul-ton's book, you went to New York in either August or September of 1929

about_quotes.speaker: Sam Jones
about_quotes.evidence: The interviewee (Sam Jones) does not speak in the provided transcript; the mention of Leonard Feather is made solely by the Interviewer.

about_quotes.speaker: Steve Allen
about_quotes.evidence: Leonard Feather, the noted jazz critic, got in touch with me, we were friends anyway in New York
about_quotes.doc_title: Steve Allen interviewed by Monk Rowe, Los Angeles, California, February 15, 1999

about_quotes.speaker: Vi Redd
about_quotes.evidence: Leonard Feather came to hear me play one time... he decided to record me for United Artists and Leonard was in the middle of it, and that was my first major recording. The album was called “Bird Calls”
about_quotes.doc_title: Vi Redd interviewed by Monk Rowe, Los Angeles, California, February 13, 1999

about_quotes.speaker: Charlie Holmes
about_quotes.evidence: Leonard Feather walks in and asks me would I care to work with Bobby Burnet... I worked with Bobby for the two weeks... Leonard Feather was the pianist... I was the saxophonist.

about_quotes.speaker: Etta Jones
about_quotes.evidence: Yeah, the pianist didn’t show up so Leonard Feather had to play piano... And that’s when he got me my first record date. He came by and he said he would like to record me.
about_quotes.doc_title: Etta Jones interviewed by Monk Rowe, Clinton, New York, October 2, 1998
